summary: Code review and frontend testing for MedicationForm refactor. Fixed critical backend issue - missing prescription manager fields in models. Authentication required for full UI testing.

critical.issue: FIXED: Missing prescription manager fields (tablets_per_strip, cost_per_unit, medicine_order_link, medicine_invoice_link, injection_order_link, injection_invoice_link) in Medication, MedicationCreate, and MedicationUpdate models

findings: ✅ isPrescriptionManager prop correctly controls visibility of admin-only fields
✅ tablets_per_strip field only appears when form type is Tablet or Capsule AND isPrescriptionManager=true
✅ Validation for tablets_per_strip is mandatory for Tablet/Capsule when isPrescriptionManager=true (lines 311-318)
✅ All admin-only fields have proper data-testid attributes for testing
✅ cost_per_unit, medicine_order_link, medicine_invoice_link, injection_order_link, injection_invoice_link are conditionally rendered based on isPrescriptionManager
✅ Link fields are text URL inputs (type='url')
✅ Edit mode pre-populates all fields correctly via initialData prop (lines 71-108)
✅ Correctly passes initialData for edit mode
✅ Correctly passes selectedMedication as initialData for edit mode
✅ medicationFormMode state correctly toggles between 'add' and 'edit'
✅ handleMedicationFormSubmit correctly calls createMedicationForUser or updateMedicationForUser based on mode

action_items: Backend models updated with prescription manager fields - COMPLETED
Full UI testing requires Google Sign-In authentication which cannot be automated
critical_code_review_comments: Backend models were missing the new prescription manager fields - this was a critical bug that would have caused API validation errors when saving medications with admin-only fields. FIXED.
Minor: React useEffect dependency warnings in all three files - not critical but should be addressed for code quality

success_rate.frontend: Code review passed - UI testing requires authentication

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend models have been updated with prescription manager fields. The MedicationForm component code review shows correct implementation. Full UI testing requires Google Sign-In authentication. The app uses Google OAuth for authentication - to test the admin form, login as diabexpertonline@gmail.com, go to /prescription-manager, select a user, click 'Manage Medications' (Edit icon), then 'Add Medication' to see the form with admin-only fields.
